title: Entrenched

Both of which are placed in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] respectively by the ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>

"'''Entrenched'''" is a 12-page comic featured in ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', written and drawn by [[Alex Jaeger]] and [[M. Zachary Sherman]].

==Publisher's summary==
"''The trenches of Hoth are the setting for this hard-hitting military action, as we witness the relentless Imperial attack from a young Rebel soldier's point-of-view.''"<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />''

==Plot summary==
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] [[Jobin]] records a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] message for his [[Parent/Legends|mother]], overlaid with his experiences in the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. In one of the trenches at [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth's]] [[Echo Station 5-7/Legends|Echo Station 5-7]], Jobin apologises for being unable to write, attributing it to the Rebellion's constant mobility and his [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] orders that they avoid writing to their homes to avoid homesickness. Jobin expresses his concern that he has no [[Time/Legends|time]] to waste given what he believes will occur. Jobin recounts recent events, such as his assignment to lead [[4th Squad]]

==Development==
{{Quote|Original story I pitched was about the ‘Imperial troops have entered the base!' Guy. I wanted to see the guys in the trenches during the battle as well as Ion control. And get to draw AT-ATs, AT-STs and snow troopers. All done in my free time while still working full time.|Alex Jaeger|<ref name="Twitter">{{Twitter|alxartdesign|status/1630940959033704448|Alex Jaeger|date=2023-03-02|quote=Original story I pitched was about the ‘Imperial troops have entered the base!' Guy. I wanted to see the guys in the trenches during the battle as well as Ion control. And get to draw AT-ATs, AT-STs and snow troopers. All done in my free time while still working full time|archivedate=20230303111335}}</ref>}}
"Entrenched" was written by [[Alex Jaeger]] and [[M. Zachary Sherman]],<ref name="Entrenched" /> and also pencilled, inked, lettered, and colored by Jaeger.<ref name="Epic Collection" /> The comic was created for ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', a graphic novel anthology that was proposed by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler|J.W. Rinzler]] as a way to share the work of concept artists who had worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] that would have otherwise gone unseen by the public.<ref name="Visionaries" /> The artists each had free reign in choosing the aspect of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe they wished to explore.<ref name="Comics">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref>

Jaeger originally pitched the idea for the comic as explaining the backstory for the line "Imperial troops have entered the base,"<ref name="Twitter" /> which is spoken over Echo Base's intercom in the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> He was motivated to write a story focused on the rebel troopers in the trenches on Hoth, while also illustrating components of the Imperial Army such as AT-ATs, AT-STs, and snowtroopers. Jaeger completed work on the comic on top of his full time work.<ref name="Twitter" /> ''Visionaries'' was announced on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 3]], [[2004]], which also revealed Jaeger's invovlement.<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> The title and a summary of "Entrenched," along with Sherman's involvement, were revealed in another StarWars.com post published on [[March 15]], 2005.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />

==Release and reception==
{{Quote|"Entrenched" by Alex Jaeger (with M. Zachary Sherman): During the Battle of Hoth, a young Alliance soldier records a message home just as the Empire invades the Rebel base.|A summary of the story published in ''Star Wars Insider'' 82|<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref>}}
''Visionaries'' was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005, prior to<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> the release of ''Revenge of the Sith'' on May 19 of that year.<ref name="Year By Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> "Entrenched" is the fifth story in the collection, following "[[Sithisis]]" and preceeding "[[The Fourth Precept]]."<ref name="Visionaries" /> Barlow praised the variety of works in ''Visionaries'', claiming that the artists involved had justified their employment by<ref name="Insider 82" /> ''Star Wars'' creator<ref name="Year By Year" /> [[George Lucas]].<ref name="Insider 82" />

The whole of ''Visionaries'' was republished within ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]'' on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "Entrenched" was later printed in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Rebellion Vol. 6]]'',<ref name="Epic Collection" /> released by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[September 10]], [[2024]].<ref name="Random House Epic Collection">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302956400|text=STAR WARS LEGENDS EPIC COLLECTION: THE REBELLION VOL. 6}}</ref> In the collection, the story is placed between the comic story "[[Fortune, Fate, and the Natural History of the Sarlacc]]," first published in [[Star Wars Tales 6|''Star Wars Tales'' 6]] in [[2000]], and ''[[Classic Star Wars: Return of the Jedi 1]]'' a [[1994]] [[Classic Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (comic series)|republication]] of Marvel Comics' [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983)|adaptation]] of the original trilogy film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], published contemporaneously with it in [[1983]].<ref name="Epic Collection" /> The Epic Collection was additionally republished in ''[[Star Wars Legends: The Rebellion Omnibus Vol. 3]]'' on [[July 15]], [[2025]].<ref name="Random House Marvel Omnibus">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302964177|text=STAR WARS LEGENDS: THE REBELLION OMNIBUS VOL. 3 HUGH FLEMING LUKE COVER}}</ref>

==Continuity==
"Entrenched" depicts the Battle of Hoth, which was originally created for ''The Empire Strikes Back'', and includes alternate perspectives<ref name="Entrenched" /> on moments from the film, including the interrupted announcement that "Imperial troops have entered the base" and the escape of the ''Millennium Falcon'' from Darth Vader.<ref name="ESB" /> The ending of the comic occurs during the events of ''Return of the Jedi'',<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> revealing that Mon Mothma discovered the death of her son shortly before her appearance<ref name="Entrenched" /> in the film.<ref name="ROTJ" /> The events of both films occurred in both [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] and [[4 ABY]] respectively, placing "Entrenched" in both of those years as well.<ref name="3 ABY" /> 

Near the end of the comic, Jobin mentions Mon Mothma's daughter Lieda,<ref name="Entrenched" /> a character who originated in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' in [[1993]]<ref name="DES">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> and her first name was first used in 2002 in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'', although her surname was not specified,<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and "Entrenched" did not establish whether Jobin and Lieda had the same father.<ref name="Entrenched" />

The Rebels set the ion cannon to self-destruct in the story,<ref name="Entrenched" /> an action previously established to have been taken by the [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] crew of the cannon for the purpose of covering the escape of the remaining transports in the [[1997]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''.<ref name="EGWT">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> and the [[2002]] magazine issue ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 22]]''<ref name="FF 22">{{FactFile|22|ION 1-2|Ion Cannon}}</ref> However, none of the sources explcitly described whether the cannon actually exploded, as it was also depicted still functioning during the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jaden Korr]]'s [[Mission to Hoth (Jaden Korr)|mission to Hoth]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'',<ref name="Jedi Academy">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''</ref> the [[Disciples of Ragnos crisis|events]] of which took place in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

On page 69, there is a mistake in Vader's line: ''were'' instead of ''where''. The error was corrected in later publications.
